object.setup_prose: As a data analyst in the Registrar's office, you are reviewing two different datasets regarding student course engagement. Dataset A tracks the survey responses of the Class of 2024 at three points: freshman year, junior year, and senior year. Dataset B provides a single survey of all currently enrolled students across every year of study, collected during the current spring semester.
object.prompt: Identify which dataset is a cohort study and which is a cross-sectional study. Explain why Dataset B cannot be used to determine how an individual student's engagement changes over the course of their Harvard career.
object.hint_content: Consider whether the dataset follows the exact same people as they age or change status, or if it provides a 'photo' of different people standing at different stages of their journey at the exact same moment.
object.rubric_primary: The learner must correctly categorize the datasets and provide a reasoning based on the difference between longitudinal tracking (following the same individuals) versus a point-in-time snapshot (capturing different individuals at different stages).

visible_thinking_prompts.notice: What differences do you see in the timeframes and student groups included in each dataset?
visible_thinking_prompts.think: What kind of conclusions can you draw about a student's experience from Dataset A versus Dataset B?
visible_thinking_prompts.wonder: What might you be missing about a student's journey if you only look at Dataset B?
